specific_mechanism: CANTOR-LR: middle-thirds removal + LR-active on Cantor subintervals + plateau on middle thirds + depth d recursion
llm_application: Build the training-step LR schedule from a recursive interval-removal construction: split [0,T] into thirds, remove the middle third, recurse on the outer thirds to depth d. LR decays on the surviving subintervals; LR is held constant during the removed middle thirds. Step count and decay rate scale with the self-similar dimension log2/log3.
novelty_claim: Agarwal-Goel-Zhang fractal LR uses Chebyshev step ordering; Cantor middle-thirds is a distinct fractal construction.
